title: erigeron alpinus subsp. intermedius
intro: Erigeron alpinus subsp. intermedius, also known as Alpine Fleabane or Intermediate Fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron alpinus subsp. intermedius include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to harness its medicinal properties.
